segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a desolate, eerie campsite under a hazy, yellow-orange sky, featuring dilapidated cabins and barren trees, setting a spooky tone. An upbeat, slightly spooky rock-style theme song with male vocals singing "Camp Lakebottom where the salt is true!" plays as the scene quickly shifts to a group of animated campers—two boys and a girl—running in terror from a monstrous, toothy creature emerging from a cabin amidst screams and monster sounds. The chaos escalates as a giant, red, lobster-like monster bursts through a window, startling a chef in a kitchen. The scene cuts to a serene lake where two girls sunbathe on a floating raft. Suddenly, the tranquility is broken by a splash as a large, skeletal hand emerges from the water, pulling one girl under with a "gulp" and a scream. The video then transitions to a dark forest where a boy and girl fight with sticks against a monstrous, skull-faced creature. The campers are shown again, fleeing in panic from a giant, toothy monster, accompanied by the theme song's lyrics "Camp Lakebottom where it's spooky and cool!" and "Camp Lakebottom don't eat that food!". The title card 'Camp Lakebottom' appears with the characters joyfully posed around it, as the theme song continues with "Camp Lakebottom bring your nightmares out into you!". The next scene shows a boy confronting a large, angry rooster in the woods, which then transforms into a monstrous 'were-chicken' with loud "Cluck-a-doodle-doo!" sounds as the episode title 'Cluck of the Were-Chicken' is displayed. The boy exclaims, "What is up with that chicken? He's been crowing all night!" before declaring, "That's it, pal! You cock-a-doodle your last doo!" as the rooster squawks and screeches. The video ends with a close-up of the boy in bed, wide-eyed and terrified, before he bolts upright and rushes out of his cabin, exclaiming "Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa!". He finds a girl standing in the doorway, looking equally alarmed, who asks, "What was that?". The boy replies, "I don't know, but it sounds like trouble!", to which the girl suggests, "We should go check it out!". The boy insists, "No, we should run!", and when the girl asks, "But what if it's a monster?", he responds, "Then we run faster!". As the girl says, "I think it's coming this way!", the boy urges, "Let's hide!", followed by a final loud "Cluck-a-doodle-doo!" and a screech. The final shot shows the boy running outside, with the girl following, as the night sky looms over the camp.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a dark, dense forest with suspenseful, low background music and rustling sounds. A boy with brown hair, later identified as McGee, a girl with black pigtails, and a large, round boy in a light green shirt are hiding among bushes. McGee appears agitated, taunting, "What's up, McGee? Get lost on the way to the outhouse again?" and complaining, "That cluckhead has woken me up every night this week!" Suddenly, a menacing Were-chicken with black feathers, a red comb, and sharp teeth emerges from the shadows, letting out a loud crow. McGee reacts with shock and fear, exclaiming, "What? This thing squawks all night and now it wants to sleep?" and defiantly stating, "Time to scramble your eggs, chicken!" The girl with black pigtails corrects him, "Actually, that's a rooster. They don't lay eggs," to which McGee dismissively replies, "Whatever." The Were-chicken then crows very loudly and menacingly, prompting McGee to taunt, "Ha! Wow, how'd you like that, bird brain?" The Were-chicken responds with an aggressive squawk and lunges, causing a chaotic explosion of dirt and debris as McGee screams in fear. Knocked to the ground and dazed with a scratch on his cheek, McGee mutters, "Ah, not so much." As the Were-chicken continues its aggressive squawks, the large boy in green shirt panics, yelling, "Guys! Careful!" and the girl with black pigtails adds, "You're scaring it!" The Were-chicken crows triumphantly as McGee regains his footing, looking angry and determined, declaring, "I better not hear another peep out of you. No chicken messes with McGee." The girl again corrects him, "Rooster," and McGee again replies, "Whatever." The large boy and the girl run away in panic, while McGee stands his ground, glaring at the Were-chicken, which emits a deep, guttural sound followed by a loud squawk. The scene then shifts to a surreal, red-tinted flashback, accompanied by ominous growling and rumbling, showing a man in overalls on a rocking chair outside a cabin that collapses as he falls, suggesting a past trauma tied to the Were-chicken’s origin, ending with more rustling, squawking, an impact sound, and a final deep roar from the Were-chicken.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a dark, ominous view of a cabin in a forest at night, framed by large, pinkish hands, accompanied by a low, ominous hum that gives way to a sharp metallic clank and a thud. The scene shifts to a rural village under a pink-tinged sky, as a large, monstrous, pink, blob-like creature with a flower on its head emerges from the ground, causing destruction with a deep, guttural growl, followed by a high-pitched, terrified scream. Inside a wooden cabin, a stern, plump woman with gray hair, wearing a white blouse and purple skirt, stands angrily in the doorway, confronting a glowing, yellow, translucent ghost-like figure with a long beard and a purple sash. She gestures aggressively, then points upward as a pale, zombie-like man with a red flashlight attached to his leg appears behind her, looking terrified, all while a low, rumbling growl is heard, followed by a thump and a squish. The scene cuts to a bedroom where the stern woman now faces three children: a girl with black pigtails in a red top, a a large boy in a green shirt, and a boy with brown hair in a blue shirt. The girl with black pigtails speaks animatedly, recounting how "Something tasted the kitchen, ate all the maters popcorn, knock soy's block off, and tried to make a sasquatch omelet." The large boy in the green shirt adds, "It was terrifying," then "And conditioning," as the woman listens with a stern expression that shifts to surprise. The glowing yellow spirit, with a deep, resonant voice, questions, "Giant egg goop? Licking the popcorn?" before dramatically declaring, "We may be dealing with... a Were-chicken!" The spirit then asks the children, "You campers didn't happen to run into a wild chicken last night, did ya?" The boy with brown hair hesitantly asks, "Does a mutant rooster count?" The glowing yellow spirit then ominously warns, "So, one of you may already carries a Were-chicken curse!" The girl with black pigtails exclaims incredulously, "A Were-chicken? That's crazy, even for Camp Lakebottom." The glowing yellow spirit cackles, "Crazy, huh?" and then dramatically instructs, "Prepare yourselves for a terrifying tale from the old country." The final scene shifts to a nighttime farmyard, where the boy with brown hair, now wearing red shorts and a white shirt, is feeding chickens. A monstrous rooster with black feathers, a red comb, and sharp teeth—the Were-chicken—emerges from the shadows with a triumphant crow, startling the boy and the chickens, as a deep voice begins, "Long ago..." and the audio fades out.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens in a dark, eerie farmyard at night with dramatic music, where a male narrator explains, "A farmer in the village of Frozen Cold was attacked by his chickens under an egg moon. That night, the farmer went through a change." A Boy with brown hair, wearing a white shirt and red shorts, is startled by a monstrous Were-chicken with black feathers, a red comb, and sharp teeth, which emerges from the shadows. The Were-chicken crows triumphantly with aggressive clucking and squawking sounds against a full moon, silhouetted dramatically, before attacking the boy, who screams in terror and flees. The narrator continues, "He became a half-man, half-chicken monster, doomed to peck the night away as a Were-chicken." The scene shifts to a town street where the Were-chicken, now with white feathers and a red comb, runs wildly, knocking over a trash can with a clatter. It then perches atop a roof, crowing again under the moonlight, as a glowing yellow spirit lies defeated nearby. The narrator reveals, "The only way to reverse the curse is a potion made with the creature's own tail feather. Yikes!" Inside a cabin, the Stern woman with gray hair, angry and confrontational, stands in the doorway, facing the Zombie-like man with flashlight and the Glowing yellow spirit, who appears to be the source of her ire. The scene cuts to a bedroom where the Stern woman, now surprised, confronts the Large boy in green shirt, the Girl with black pigtails, and the Boy with brown hair. The boy nervously stutters, "But... but... but...", while the girl with pigtails excitedly exclaims, "Woohoo!" The Stern woman, with an angry and determined voice, declares, "We're not taking the blame for that psycho cluckhead! Gear up, bottom dwellers! We're going on a chicken hunt! Rooster! Whatever!" The boy then becomes determined, clenches his fist, raises his arm in defiance, and states, "We can do this!" The group, now dressed in medieval-style armor with clanking sounds, stands outside the cabin, ready for battle. The final shot shows the Girl with orange hair and the Girl with blonde hair, both smiling and laughing joyfully on a raft with triumphant music, with the Girl with orange hair playfully holding a purple object, suggesting a shift to a lighthearted moment after the chaos.

segments.integrated_caption: The adventure begins with a close-up on McGee, a boy with spiky brown hair, wearing a blue shirt and a gray metal helmet, holding a hockey stick with a mischievous grin. He's joined by a large, round boy in a green shirt, also helmeted and holding a spatula, and Gretchen, a girl with black pigtails in a red top and striped leggings, wearing a mouse-ear helmet, as they stand outside a rustic wooden cabin in a forested area, ready for their quest. The group is called out by names like "Butt Squad!", "McGee!", "Suzy!", "Gretchen!", and "Squirt!". The scene cuts to Suzy, a blonde girl in a pink top and tiara, beside an enthusiastic boy with orange hair, who proudly displays a purple poster featuring a treasure map with coins and the text 'Treasure Map' and 'X marks the spot', promoting "Quickam Sunny Smiles 3D Monster Movie Night!". Suzy, initially unimpressed, then smiles broadly, clearly excited, though she later taunts with a sarcastic tone, "Oh, but it says here no bottom dwellers. Too bad!" McGee defiantly retorts, "Wild horses couldn't drag me to your stupid movie!", to which the boy with orange hair excitedly responds, "We've got horses!". As adventurous orchestral music begins, the group moves into a darker, eerie forest. McGee and the large boy carry a large sack and a bowl of popcorn, respectively. McGee, increasingly frustrated, exclaims, "This popcorn is great!" as the large boy loudly chews, prompting McGee to ask, "Will you please stop eating the bait?" The large boy confirms, "Mmm, it is good." A dramatic sound effect signals a shift as McGee declares, "Alright! Time to catch ourselves a witch chicken!" His frustration mounts as he adds, "We'd have more luck if you weren't still loudly chewing popcorn!" Suddenly, McGee drops the bowl, spilling popcorn, and looks angry. The scene shifts to a close-up of a small, blue-furred squirrel with red eyes, making chittering sounds as it sniffs the spilled popcorn. The group watches, their expressions shifting from annoyance to curiosity as the squirrel begins to eat, all within the dimly lit, greenish, mysterious forest, where their medieval-style helmets hint at a playful, fantasy-themed quest.

He looks defeated and exhausted, pleading, "Come on, you guys, this is crazy! I'm not the Were-chicken!" The woman stands over him, holding the log, her expression stern and unyielding, sarcastically questioning, "Oh, sure. Just taking a midnight tiara stroll, will we?" The Boy with brown hair, still trapped, defends himself, "I woke up hungry, went for popcorn and found Susie's crown thingy." The woman, addressing him as "Mikey," calmly begins, "Relax, Mikey. The moment you turn chicken, I think you it's my..." before the audio cuts off.

segments.integrated_caption: In a dimly lit, eerie outdoor setting near a chain-link cage, a Stern woman with gray hair, appearing angry, holds a small purple vial. With a somewhat high-pitched, theatrical voice, she explains, "And it's blood, no, tail fur, sir, and pop it in this bottle to finish the antidote. Easy peasy." She continues, "And if the antidote doesn't work, we make hearty camper macchi noodle soup. Also, easy peasy." Inside the cage, a Boy with brown hair, wearing a blue shirt and gray metal helmet, grins mischievously, then, with a young, mischievous voice, remarks, "Mmm, that sounds yummy." The woman sharply exclaims, "What?" as the boy laughs. She then cries, "He's changing!" as a screeching sound accompanies his transformation. The Zombie-like man with flashlight, with a gaunt, slightly whiny voice, asks, "Ooh, is it time to get my camera?" The boy laughs again. The Large boy in green shirt, with a deep, slightly exasperated voice, tries to calm the situation, "Come on, guys, it was just a piece of popcorn." The Zombie-like man and Large boy both sound disappointed. The Stern woman ominously states, "It's only a matter of time," as the boy growls and roars. The Zombie-like man panics, "Oh no!" The woman urgently commands, "Stay back!" as the boy continues to growl. The Zombie-like man panics further, "I think I'm starting to change! Here it comes!" The boy growls, and the Zombie-like man exclaims, "Ew!" The Large boy calmly observes, "Buttery." The Boy with brown hair triumphantly declares, "Gotcha!" and laughs, adding, "I couldn't resist." The Girl with black pigtails suddenly lunges forward, yelling with effort, swinging her stick with a whoosh. The Stern woman, now angry and determined, shouts, "Look! You're not going to fall for another one of your tricks!" The boy defiantly responds, "No joke this time!" The woman commands, "Turn around!" as the boy laughs. The girl continues to lunge and yell with effort, her stick whooshing, while the boy repeatedly laughs mischievously. The scene ends with the girl falling to the ground, her stick breaking with a cracking sound, as she looks up in shock and pain, all under a dark, foreboding night sky with faint stars, enhancing the tense atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with a male voice complaining that "rather could never write a monster chicken!" just as a monstrous Were-chicken bursts through the drive-in movie screen in a flash of light, letting out a loud "WAAAH!" screech. The audience, including the Girl with blonde hair and the Boy with orange hair wearing 3D glasses and clutching popcorn, reacts with terror amidst screams and tearing sounds. The Boy with brown hair, initially determined, is knocked to the ground, his expression shifting to shock and fear as suspenseful music plays and the creature growls. He then appears holding a small purple vial emitting a glowing green substance, and calmly offers the Were-chicken some glowing green popcorn, asking, "You want some popcorn? It's got special toppings!" The creature snatches and devours it with chewing sounds, momentarily calming its aggressive "WAAAH!" roars. A male voice asks, "Gretchen, you okay?" to the Girl with black pigtails, who then enthusiastically exclaims, "You've got to try this popcorn!" The Boy with orange hair comments, "The movie was lame, but that 3D was intense." The Girl with blonde hair, now annoyed, whines, "How come Nerdlinger or Gretchen gets to be in a movie, and I don't?" referring to the Boy with brown hair and Girl with black pigtails, before confidently stating, "I'd make a way better chicken!" and imitating a chicken with "cluck cluck cluck" sounds. The Were-chicken, with black feathers, red comb, and sharp teeth, is shown in close-up with wide eyes and bared teeth, making aggressive sounds again as chaos resumes. The Girl with black pigtails, now holding the glowing popcorn bag, smirks mischievously, suggesting she orchestrated the chaos. The Boy with brown hair and the Girl with black pigtails stand together in a purple-tinted moment, seemingly triumphant, before confronting the distressed Large boy in green shirt, at whom the Girl with black pigtails points accusingly. The Large boy in green shirt comments, "Man, that is one crazy chicken. Rooster." The entire scene unfolds at a nighttime drive-in with a starry sky, large teal chairs, and a glowing movie screen, creating a surreal and tense atmosphere.
